message:
fix(amplitude): deserialize API responses into typed structs (br-csgvd)

P3 — connectors/amplitude/src/client.rs::handle_response previously
returned `serde_json::Value` for every endpoint. An upstream that
altered its response shape would slip through the connector boundary
unnoticed: nothing on the wire would catch a renamed field or a removed
key until a downstream consumer dereferenced into nothing.

Typed structs ChartQueryResponse / CohortsListResponse /
EventsExportResponse already existed in types.rs (with their own unit
tests) but were not used at the API boundary.

- handle_response<T: DeserializeOwned + Default> is now generic; line
  117's `serde_json::from_str(&body)?` deserializes into the
  caller-declared type rather than into Value.
- request_with_retry<T> and get<T> propagate the generic.
- query_chart / list_cohorts / export_events declare their typed
  response (ChartQueryResponse / CohortsListResponse /
  EventsExportResponse) so a shape regression surfaces as a
  serde_json Decode error at the boundary.
- Empty-body 200s use T::default() (Default added to the three response
  structs alongside `#[serde(default)]` on their inner Vec / Value
  fields so legitimate empty payloads still parse).
- The connector's invoke_charts_query / invoke_cohorts_list /
  invoke_events_export wrap each typed response with serde_json::to_value
  to preserve the FCP wire shape.
- handle_self_check now reads response.cohorts.len() directly instead
  of round-tripping through Value::as_array.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-amplitude — 222/222 passing tests still
pass. Two unrelated pre-existing failures (config_clone,
provisioning_readiness_custom_base_url_rejected) are fallout from a
sibling base_url policy fix that did not update those tests; both fail
identically on `git stash` before my changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
